Fetal Infant Mortality Review (FIMR) Program

Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health
Fetal Infant Mortality Review (FIMR) Program
What is FIMR?
FIMR is a process that helps create new ideas to improve services for women, infants, and families in Solano County. It is a collective group of professionals from sectors including public health, healthcare, social services, community-based organizations, and more. This group reviews select fetal and infant deaths from Solano County with the goal of improving birth outcomes. FIMR is not meant to find fault or cast blame. It provides valuable information about changes in the health care system and how those changes affect real families. Additionally, it helps health officials develop and enforce public health policies to protect families.
Resources and Information
FIMR is supported by organizations such as the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG), the March of Dimes, and the federal Maternal and Child Health Bureau. More information can be found on
The National Center for Fatality Review and Prevention.
